verdict_reason: The single disagreement (kaśmala) is well-explained. It identifies the contested term, names the specific positions (Prabhupada's moral-impurity reading vs. Mukundananda's cognitive/delusion reading), specifies the wide semantic range (physical dirt, mental impurity, moral defilement, faintheartedness), and is doctrinally substantive — different readings produce different diagnoses of what Arjuna's condition IS, which is load-bearing for the chapter's voice. Names the axis (translation-register choice that turns on a metaphysical commitment). One tight line. Does not pick a winner. Gold-standard quality.
